<!-- Copilot mirror of commands/ticket-new.md. Generated by bin/sync-copilot-prompts.
     Edit the Claude source, not this file — regenerate instead. -->

> **Plane MCP pre-flight (Copilot — check this before the Plane path steps below):**
>
> Scan your available tool list for at least one tool matching `mcp__plane__*`, `plane_*`, or `mcp_plane_*`.
> - **None found → STOP.** Print: "Error: Plane MCP tools are not accessible. In VS Code, open the MCP panel (View → MCP Servers), ensure the `plane` server shows as Connected, and retry. Do not fall back to creating markdown tickets." Do not continue to the Markdown path.
> - **Found → note the active prefix** (e.g. `mcp__plane__` or `plane_`) and use it for every tool call in this session.
>
> **If `.claude/plane-config.md` exists, the Markdown path is off-limits** regardless of MCP status — never silently degrade to local TKT-*.md files when the project uses Plane.


# Create a New Ticket

You are creating a new ticket in the current project. The user will describe a bug, feature, or enhancement.

## Pre-flight: detect backend

Dispatch based on which config files exist:

- If `.claude/plane-config.md` exists → **Plane backend** (go to "Plane path").
- Else if `.claude/ticket-config.md` exists and `tickets/` exists → **markdown backend** (go to "Markdown path").
- Else: project not bootstrapped. Tell the user to run `/ticket-install` and stop.


## Plane path

### Steps

1. **Read `.claude/plane-config.md`.** Extract:
   - `Project ID` (UUID)
   - `Project identifier` (e.g. `SMOKE`)
   - State ID map (need `Backlog` for this command)
   - Label ID map (need `plan-ticket`, `app:*` for this command)
   - Preview profiles (name + whether one is marked default)

2. **Parse arguments.** Flags can appear anywhere; strip them all before treating the remainder as the free-form title + description. Relevant flags:

   *Parent (hierarchical, at most one):*
   - `--no-parent` — force standalone (skip parent inference from the current branch)
   - `--parent IDENT-N` — link to the specified work item as parent (e.g. `--parent RJG-101`)

   *Relations (any number, repeat to add more):*
   - `--follow-up IDENT-N` — `relates_to` the named work item ("this is a follow-on to that one")
   - `--blocks IDENT-N` — the new ticket `blocks` the named work item
   - `--blocked-by IDENT-N` — the new ticket is `blocked_by` the named work item
   - `--duplicate-of IDENT-N` — `duplicate` of the named work item (does NOT auto-close the new ticket; user can `/ticket-close` if they want)

   All identifier flags accept the project-prefix form (e.g. `RJG-125`).

3. **Determine parent plan ticket** (unless `--no-parent`):
   - Read the current branch: `git branch --show-current`.
   - Parse a Plane work-item identifier out of it. Accept `SMOKE-12`, `smoke-12`, `SMOKE-12-slug`, `feat/SMOKE-12`, `feat/SMOKE-12-slug`. The identifier is `<project_identifier>-<sequence_id>`.
   - If no identifier found → no parent (standalone).
   - If identifier found → call `mcp__plane__retrieve_work_item_by_identifier(project_identifier, sequence_id, expand="labels")`. The result has a `parent` field (UUID or null).
     - If `parent` is non-null → use that UUID as the new ticket's parent.
     - If `parent` is null → the current ticket has no parent plan ticket → standalone.
   
   If `--parent SMOKE-N`: call `retrieve_work_item_by_identifier` on that identifier, use its `id` as parent. Don't validate that it has the `plan-ticket` label (user may want any parent); just link.

4. **Gather info from the user's input**:
   - Title (short, descriptive; first line of the argument)
   - Type: bug, feature, or enhancement (infer from context; prepend to description as `**Type:** <type>` since CE has no dedicated type field)
   - Priority: `urgent`, `high`, `medium`, `low`, or `none` (infer from context; default `medium`; map old-vocab `critical → urgent`)
   - Description body
   - Acceptance criteria (infer from context; render as a markdown bullet list in the description)
   - **Visual context** (only if the user pasted one or more images into the prompt): for each image, write 1–3 concrete, factual sentences capturing what it shows — UI element + state + visible text + any error or anomaly. The binary image is NOT uploaded to Plane; this prose is the only artifact downstream agents (e.g. `/ticket-investigate`) will see, so it must stand alone (no "see screenshot above"). If the image contradicts or sharpens the user's prose, prefer the image and say so explicitly.
   - **App / preview profile**: check `.claude/plane-config.md` for preview profiles.
     - 2+ profiles: use AskUserQuestion. Show profile names + one-line description; default selection = profile marked default.
     - 1 profile: use it silently.
     - 0 profiles: no `app:*` label.

5. **Build the description HTML.** Structure:
   ```
   <p><strong>Type:</strong> {type}</p>
   <p>{description body}</p>
   <p><strong>Visual context</strong></p>          <!-- omit this block if no images were attached -->
   <ul><li>{image 1 description}</li>…</ul>
   <p><strong>Acceptance criteria</strong></p>
   <ul><li>{criterion 1}</li>…</ul>
   ```

6. **Create the work item.** Call `mcp__plane__create_work_item`:
   - `project_id` (from config)
   - `name` = title
   - `state` = Backlog state ID
   - `priority` = mapped priority
   - `description_html` = the built HTML
   - `parent` = parent UUID if determined, else omit
   - `labels` = list of label IDs, including `app:<profile>` if selected

   Capture the new work item's `id` (UUID) — it's needed for relations.

6a. **Resolve and create relations.** Two sources of relation targets:

   *Explicit flags* (from step 2):

   | Flag | Relation type |
   |---|---|
   | `--follow-up X` | `relates_to` |
   | `--blocks X` | `blocking` |
   | `--blocked-by X` | `blocked_by` |
   | `--duplicate-of X` | `duplicate` |

   For each flag occurrence, resolve `X` (an `IDENT-N` identifier) to its UUID via `mcp__plane__retrieve_work_item_by_identifier`. **Fail-loud on 404** with the same rule as `--parent`: abort with "Relation target X not found. Check the identifier, or drop `--<flag> X` to skip."

   *Implicit detection from prose:*

   Scan the title + description body (the free-form text the user gave you) for `\b{IDENT}-\d+\b` patterns where `{IDENT}` is THIS project's identifier from `plane-config.md` (e.g. `RJG-125`). Do NOT scan for legacy `TKT-NN` patterns — auto-resolution from the old IDs is unreliable; the user should switch to the new identifier or use `--follow-up` explicitly. For each unique match: create a `relates_to` relation. **Fail-soft on 404** (warn one line, continue) — implicit references are best-effort.

   Dedupe across both sources, and exclude:
   - The new work item itself (if the user typed its identifier in the prose, somehow).
   - The parent UUID determined in step 3 (parent is a stronger semantic than relates_to; don't double-link).
   - Any target already linked via a stronger explicit flag in this same call.

   For each remaining `(target_uuid, relation_type)` pair, call:
   ```
   mcp__plane__create_work_item_relation(
     project_id=<project UUID>,
     work_item_id=<new ticket UUID>,
     relation_type=<type>,
     issues=[<target UUID>]
   )
   ```

   Collect a list of `(target_identifier, target_name, relation_type)` tuples for the output summary.

7. **Output a summary.** Build it from the cases below.

   **Header (always):**
   ```
   Created {project_identifier}-{sequence_id}: {title}
   Priority: {priority} | State: Backlog | Parent: {parent_identifier or "(none)"}
   ```

   **Relations block (only if step 6a created any):**
   ```
   Relations:
     {target1_identifier} {(relation_type1)}  — {target1_name}
     {target2_identifier} {(relation_type2)}  — {target2_name}
   ```

   **Footer (always):**
   ```
   Next: run /ticket-investigate {project_identifier}-{sequence_id} to begin investigation.
   ```

   If parent was inferred from branch (no `--parent` or `--no-parent` passed), append before the footer:
   ```
   Use /tn --no-parent "..." next time to create standalone.
   ```

### Rules (Plane path)

- New tickets always go to state `Backlog`.
- Do NOT start investigating yet — just create the work item.
- Do NOT set `risk:*` label at creation; that's `/ticket-investigate`'s job.
- If branch parsing finds a ticket identifier but `retrieve_work_item_by_identifier` 404s, fall back to standalone and print a one-line warning.
- **`--parent X` fails loud on 404:** abort with "Parent ticket X not found. Check the identifier, or drop `--parent` to create standalone." (Branch inference is implicit, so it fails-soft; explicit flags are a user commitment and must fail-loud.)
- **Explicit relation flags (`--follow-up`, `--blocks`, `--blocked-by`, `--duplicate-of`) fail loud on 404** with the same rule. The user committed to that link.
- **Implicit relations from prose fail soft on 404:** print one warning line ("could not resolve {ID} mentioned in description; skipping link"), continue with the rest. The new work item itself is already created — never roll back the create over a relation failure.
- `--duplicate-of X` does NOT auto-close the new ticket; if you want to close it, run `/ticket-close` after.
- If `--parent X` and `--follow-up X` (or any other relation flag) name the same target, the parent link wins; skip the redundant `relates_to` link silently.
- **Pasted images are session-only.** The Plane MCP exposes no attachment-upload tool, so any image the user pasted into the prompt is dropped at session end. Always distill it into the Visual context block (step 4 / step 5) so the next agent has the information in text form.


## Markdown path

*(Unchanged from the pre-Plane workflow. Kept in-tree for projects that haven't been migrated to Plane yet. Will be removed when Plan 3 completes.)*

### Steps

1. **Read the project's ticket config** at `.claude/ticket-config.md` to learn the tickets directory and ID prefix (default: `tickets/` and `TKT-`).

2. **Determine the next ticket ID**:
   - List files **recursively** under the tickets directory matching `{PREFIX}*.md` — this MUST include terminal subfolders (`shipped/`, `deferred/`, `wontfix/`) so IDs are never reused for tickets that have been moved out of the active set.
   - Find the highest number across all matches and increment by 1.
   - If no tickets exist, start at `{PREFIX}001`.
   - The new ticket file is always created at the root of the tickets directory (`{tickets-dir}/{PREFIX}{NNN}.md`), never in a subfolder.

3. **Gather info from the user's input** (the argument to this command):
   - Title (short, descriptive)
   - Type: bug, feature, or enhancement
   - Priority: low, medium, high, or critical (infer from context, default medium)
   - Description
   - Acceptance criteria (infer from the description if not explicit)
   - **Visual context** (only if the user pasted one or more images into the prompt): for each image, write 1–3 concrete, factual sentences capturing what it shows — UI element + state + visible text + any error or anomaly. The binary image is NOT saved with the ticket; this prose is the only artifact downstream agents (e.g. `/ticket-investigate`) will see, so it must stand alone (no "see screenshot above"). If the image contradicts or sharpens the user's prose, prefer the image and say so explicitly.
   - **App / preview profile**: read the `## Preview profiles` section of `.claude/ticket-config.md`. If there are 2+ profiles, use AskUserQuestion to ask which one this ticket should target (show the profile names + a one-line description for each; default selection = the profile marked `default: true`). If there's only 1 profile, use it silently. If there are no profiles, set `app: (none)`.

4. **Create the ticket file** at `{tickets-dir}/{PREFIX}{NNN}.md` using `{tickets-dir}/TEMPLATE.md`:
   - Fill in all header fields (id, title, type, status: open, priority, created date, updated date)
   - Fill in Description and Acceptance Criteria. If images were attached, append a `**Visual context**` subsection at the end of the Description with one bullet per image.
   - Leave all agent sections empty

5. **Output a summary**:
   ```
   Created {ID}: {title}
   Type: {type} | Priority: {priority} | Status: open

   Next: run /ticket-investigate {ID} to begin investigation
   ```

### Rules (markdown path)

- Status must be `open` for new tickets
- Branch field is empty until investigation/approval
- Do NOT start investigating yet — just create the ticket
- Use today's date for created/updated
- **Pasted images are session-only.** The ticket file stores text only — any image the user pasted into the prompt is dropped at session end. Always distill it into the Visual context bullets (step 3 / step 4) so the next agent has the information in text form.

## Compatibility Notes

- **MCP tool mapping:** In VS Code Copilot, Plane MCP tools are typically exposed as `plane_<tool_name>` (e.g. `plane_create_work_item`), not `mcp__plane__<tool_name>` (Claude Code convention). The Plane MCP pre-flight above detects which prefix is active and uses it for the session. For explicit IDs, prefer direct identifier lookup; if unavailable, use bounded project-scoped lookup + exact match.
- **AskUserQuestion:** Copilot has no equivalent structured-question tool. When the Claude source says "use AskUserQuestion", ask the user in chat with a numbered or bulleted choice and wait for a reply.
- **Parallel subagents → sequential loop:** if the Claude source fans out subagents in parallel (e.g. ticket-investigate multi-ticket, ticket-batch), run them as a sequential loop in one agent session instead. Output and behavior are identical; only wall-clock differs.
- **Backend dispatch:** the "Pre-flight: detect backend" block is authoritative in both hosts. `.claude/plane-config.md` presence routes to the Plane path; otherwise the Markdown path runs.
